2026-02-20.name: Advancing Emergency Drone Control Through Semantic Voice Perception
2026-02-20.description: This episode explores a paradigm shift toward specialized speech technology, focusing on the Voice-Driven Semantic Perception for UAV-Assisted Emergency Networks paper. We discuss how intent-driven communication enables drone control in disaster zones while highlighting new fine-tuned models for Swahili and Amharic by developers like korir8 and amanuelbyte. Finally, we examine how CoreML and Moonshine optimizations are bringing high-performance, private ASR directly to edge devices and smartphon

2026-02-19.name: Advancing Global Speech Coverage Through Community Model Adaptation
2026-02-19.description: Explore how the global community is democratizing ASR by adapting foundation models like Meta’s MMS and OpenAI’s Whisper for underrepresented languages. We discuss Vitthal Bhandari’s fine-tunes for African languages alongside iterative Welsh model improvements from DewiBrynJones. Discover how specialized tools like faster-whisper-tilsync are shifting the focus from general transcription toward precise audio-text synchronization for real-world media applications.

2026-02-18.name: Scaling Spoken Language Models with Zero Resource Syllable Tokenization
2026-02-18.description: This episode explores ZeroSyl, a breakthrough in zero-resource syllable tokenization that drastically reduces sequence lengths for more efficient spoken language modeling. We also discuss the shift toward edge AI with quantized Nemotron-Speech models on the MLX framework for high-speed local inference. Finally, we highlight community-driven innovations like the MMS adaptation for Fongbe and IPA-specialized models for nuanced phonetic recognition.

2026-02-17.name: Unified Multimodal Models Driving Real-Time Speech Understanding Breakthroughs
2026-02-17.description: This episode explores Mistral AI’s release of Voxtral-Mini-4B-Realtime-2602, a unified model designed to eliminate latency by processing audio and language tokens simultaneously. We also examine groundbreaking research on CLAP-Based Automatic Word Naming Recognition, which leverages contrastive learning to improve ASR accuracy for patients with post-stroke aphasia. This deep dive highlights how localized fine-tuning for languages like Urdu and Arabic dialects is outperforming general-purpose mod

2026-02-16.name: Advancing Inclusive Speech Recognition Through Targeted Model Adaptation
2026-02-16.description: This episode explores the transition from general ASR to inclusive, efficient models tailored for underserved communities. We analyze the massive growth of the Pashto Common Voice dataset and the critical need for contributor diversity to avoid algorithmic bias. Additionally, we examine the Akan Impaired Speech Dataset and the DAMA framework, which enables high-performance adaptation using eighty percent fewer trainable parameters for low-resource languages.

2026-02-22.name: The Industry Pivot Toward Unified Native Speech Language Models
2026-02-22.description: This episode explores the current industry shift from traditional cascading pipelines toward unified, speech-native architectures like Sonic-7B and Whisper-v5. We examine how discrete neural audio codecs and semantic-acoustic decoupling enable sub-200ms latency for more natural, real-time conversational AI. Discover why practitioners are moving away from the old ASR and TTS approach in favor of sophisticated on-device adaptation and state space models like Mamba.

2026-02-21.name: Scaling Precision ASR with Localized and Efficient Edge Models
2026-02-21.description: This episode explores the transition from massive general-purpose models to Precision ASR through localized tools like the Qwen3-ASR-0.6B-Albanian model. We discuss how specialized architectures and the whisper-large-v3-Tarteel-even-g2 fine-tune are bridging the gap for low-resource languages and unique phonetic structures. Finally, we examine the practical benefits of medasr-onnx for private medical transcription and why K-means clustering is making a comeback in modern speech tokenization.

2026-W08.name: Transitioning From Transcription To Native Audio Understanding Models
2026-W08.description: This episode explores the shift toward native audio processing with Mistral AI’s Voxtral-Mini-4B-Realtime-2602, which integrates speech and language for low-latency interactions. We also dive into clinical breakthroughs for disordered speech using the CLAP framework and discuss how DAMA enables efficient model adaptation for low-resource languages like Pashto. Join us as we examine why the future of ASR focuses on intent recognition rather than just word error rates.

qwen3-asr-technical-report-deep-dive.name: Advancing Multilingual Speech Recognition with Qwen3-ASR Foundation Models
qwen3-asr-technical-report-deep-dive.description: This deep dive explores the Qwen3-ASR technical report and its revolutionary approach to multilingual speech recognition and forced alignment. We examine how the 1.7B and 0.6B models leverage the Qwen3-Omni foundation to outperform existing systems in complex scenarios like singing voice recognition and diverse Chinese dialects. The discussion highlights technical innovations such as the AuT encoder architecture and the implementation of Group Sequence Policy Optimization to ensure production-re

qwen3-omni-technical-report-deep-dive.name: Breaking Audio Benchmarks with the Qwen3-Omni Thinker-Talker Architecture
qwen3-omni-technical-report-deep-dive.description: This episode explores the groundbreaking Qwen3-Omni technical report, a multimodal model from Alibaba that eliminates performance trade-offs across text, audio, and video. We dive into the Thinker-Talker MoE design and the new Audio Transformer encoder trained on twenty million hours of data to achieve state-of-the-art ASR results. Learn how the Code2Wav convolutional network achieves incredible sub-250ms latency for real-time conversational AI applications.

robust-speech-recognition-via-large-scal.name: Scaling Robust Speech Recognition via Large Scale Weak Supervision
robust-speech-recognition-via-large-scal.description: In this episode, we dive into the landmark OpenAI paper titled "Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision," which introduced the world to Whisper. We explore how 680,000 hours of internet data helped overcome the fragility of models like wav2vec 2.0 and LibriSpeech-tuned systems. The discussion covers the standard encoder-decoder architecture, the clever use of multitask prompt tokens, and the practical challenges of hallucinations in zero-shot environments.

hubert-self-supervised-speech-representa.name: Transforming Continuous Audio into Discrete Units with HuBERT
hubert-self-supervised-speech-representa.description: Explore the groundbreaking HuBERT paper from Meta, which introduced a novel approach to self-supervised speech representation learning. We discuss how the authors used K-means clustering to discretize audio, moving beyond the contrastive learning methods seen in wav2vec 2.0. This conversation highlights HuBERT's impressive performance on the Librispeech benchmark and its influence on later generative models like AudioLM and VALL-E.

tac-timestamped-audio-captioning-deep-di.name: Eliminating Hallucinations in Audio Language Models with TAC
tac-timestamped-audio-captioning-deep-di.description: In this episode, we examine TAC: Timestamped Audio Captioning, a new model from Adobe Research designed to eliminate hallucinations in large audio language models. Our guest explains how the Dynamic Acoustic Mixer generates synthetic training data with precise temporal labels to solve the semantic collapse seen in models like Audio Flamingo 3. We also discuss the describe-then-reason paradigm and the TAC-V variant, which sets new benchmarks for multimodal reasoning and dense audio-visual underst

2026-02-23.name: Deploying High Efficiency Transducer Models on Mobile Hardware via CoreML
2026-02-23.description: Explore the shift toward edge-optimized speech technology with an in-depth look at NVIDIA’s Parakeet-TDT CoreML adaptation for high-efficiency mobile transcription. This conversation examines how specialized releases like the phonetic ipa-whisper-medium and multimodal Voxtral-Mini-4B are transforming ASR from simple text output into nuanced, context-aware interfaces. We also highlight community-driven breakthroughs in low-resource languages, including new fine-tunes for Bemba and rhythmic adapta

csm-crossing-the-uncanny-valley-of-conve.name: Crossing the Uncanny Valley with Conversational Speech Models
csm-crossing-the-uncanny-valley-of-conve.description: This episode explores Sesame Research's groundbreaking paper on the Conversational Speech Model and its mission to bridge the uncanny valley of human-AI dialogue. We analyze the technical architecture of the 8-billion parameter multimodal backbone, the integration of the Mimi codec, and the dual-Whisper strategy used to process one million hours of training data. Discover how innovations like compute amortization enable these models to capture the subtle nuances of emotional context and social p

benchmark-datasets-deep-dive.name: ASR Benchmark Deep Dive: Choosing Models by Dataset, Not Averages
benchmark-datasets-deep-dive.description: A benchmark-first technical podcast episode covering what each major ASR dataset measures and how model rankings shift across LibriSpeech, Common Voice, VoxPopuli, TED-LIUM, GigaSpeech, SPGISpeech, Earnings22, and AMI. Includes practical guidance for selecting models for meeting transcription, finance, and open-domain workloads.

2026-02-24.name: Reducing Latency in Generative Speech with Dual Streaming Alignment
2026-02-24.description: This episode explores how new architectures like CTC-TTS use dual-streaming frameworks and CTC alignment to achieve human-like prosody without the typical latency of LLM-based speech synthesis. We also dive into Cross-lingual Matryoshka Representation Learning, which enables efficient semantic retrieval for oral languages like Wolof by packing essential data into smaller embedding dimensions. Finally, the discussion covers a massive wave of community releases on Hugging Face, including Qwen3-ASR

2026-02-25.name: Advancing Low Resource Speech Recognition with Translation Guided Learning
2026-02-25.description: This episode explores the frontier of speech technology, focusing on how researchers are bridging the gap for low-resource languages through smarter architectures. We discuss the TG-ASR framework which uses Mandarin subtitles to train Hokkien models and the use of Punctuation Restoration Modules to improve Nepali-to-English translation pipelines. From Multimodal Large Language Models to community fine-tunes of Whisper-large-v3, discover how cross-modal signals and synthetic data are transforming

2026-03-09.name: Advancing Global Speech Technology through Localized Model Specialization
2026-03-09.description: This deep dive explores the transition from general-purpose ASR to specialized, localized models that prioritize hardware efficiency and noise robustness. We discuss the breakthrough integration of visual signals in MSP-Visual-AV-HuBERT and the expansion of linguistic diversity through the AfriSpeech dataset and Egyptian Arabic diarization. The conversation also covers the engineering shift toward local inference using ONNX quantization and Apple’s MLX framework for privacy-first, low-latency sp
